data.title: Gnosis Chain: xDAI bridge will use USDS as the default token on the Ethereum chain, and the migration is expected to take effect on June 8
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Gnosis Chain posted on the X platform that third-party applications need to take action, and the xDAI bridge contract is undergoing a key upgrade: on Ethereum, DAI will be replaced by USDS as the default accepted token, and xDAI will continue to be minted on Gnosis Chain. Third-party applications must complete integration with the new Bridge Router contract on Ethereum before June 8, 2025.

data.title: Union Restores Wallet Functionality Amid Ongoing Monitoring
data.description: According to Foresight News, Union, a modular interoperability layer, has announced that its wallet functionality has been restored. Engineers are actively monitoring the situation to ensure continued stability. This follows an earlier disclosure by Union, made one hour prior, regarding an anomaly in the wallet function that was under repair.

data.title: Revolut to launch Bitcoin Lightning Network payments in European markets
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Digital bank Revolut announced that it will cooperate with Lightspark to launch a payment function based on the Bitcoin Lightning Network in the UK and some European Economic Area countries. This move aims to use Lightspark's payment infrastructure to reduce transaction fees and speed up payment processing. Lightspark is a company led by former PayPal executive David Marcus, focusing on building global payment solutions based on the Bitcoin Lightning Network. At present, Revolut has not announced the specific launch time of this function. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Polygon Labs Launches CDK OP Stack Configuration
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Polygon Labs announced that its chain development toolkit CDK (now renamed Agglayer CDK) officially supports multiple technology stacks, with the first integration being the OP Stack configuration. The new version allows developers to use the OP Stack to launch high-performance, low-cost chains and connect natively to Agglayer without paying extra fees. In the future, Arbitrum Orbit, ABC Stack, etc. may also be connected to the Agglayer ecosystem.

data.title: World Liberty Financial to Airdrop USD1 Stablecoin to WLFI Token Holders
data.description:
TL;DR Exclusive Airdrop: World Liberty Financial will distribute its new USD1 stablecoin exclusively to WLFI token holders as a token of appreciation and to test the platform’s on-chain distribution system. Community-Backed Innovation: The governance proposal passed with nearly 100% support, aiming to validate smart contract functionality and ensure robust, secure distribution. Path to Future Enhancements:.
source: https://crypto-economy.com/world-liberty-financial-to-airdrop-usd1-stablecoin-to-wlfi-token-holders/

data.title: U.S. and Israel Discuss Potential U.S.-Led Governance in Gaza
data.description: According to BlockBeats, sources have revealed that the United States and Israel are in discussions regarding the possibility of establishing a U.S.-led government in Gaza. This development comes amid ongoing geopolitical tensions in the region. The talks are reportedly focused on exploring governance options that could stabilize the area and address security concerns. Further details on the discussions have not been disclosed.

data.title: Opinion: If Trump imposes comprehensive tariffs, Russia may become the main beneficiary of BTC mining
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News: Russian Bitcoin mining technology company Luxor predicts that if Trump imposes comprehensive tariffs on the Bitcoin industry supply chain, Russia will become the main beneficiary, because most Bitcoin mining hardware is still designed outside the United States. The industry has long been dominated by Antminer, a subsidiary of Bitmain, with a market share of more than 80%.
In addition, Bitcoin mining activities in Russia have grown significantly, benefiting from energy cost advantages and the gradual relaxation of cryptocurrency regulations. Despite facing sanctions from Western countries, the Russian government is pushing to legalize cryptocurrencies, especially for international payments and mining. Experts point out that despite Russia's progress in the cryptocurrency field, it remains challenging to use cryptocurrencies to circumvent sanctions on a large scale due to market liquidity constraints. (The Block)

data.title: Michael Saylor Urges Microsoft to Reconsider Capital Allocation Strategy
data.description:
According to PANews, Michael Saylor, Chairman of the Board at Strategy (formerly MicroStrategy), has called on Microsoft to revise its capital allocation strategy during the 'Strategy World 2025' summit. Saylor suggested that Microsoft should cease investing in low-yield bonds and stock buybacks, advocating instead for Bitcoin to become a central option in the company's asset allocation.

Data indicates that over the past five years, Bitcoin has achieved an annualized return of 53%, with a cumulative increase of over 950%. In contrast, Microsoft's stock has seen an annualized return of 6% and a cumulative rise of approximately 148% during the same period. Saylor criticized Microsoft's use of cash flow for what he termed inefficient capital operations. He argued that if the $200 billion returned to shareholders over the past five years had been invested in Bitcoin, the company's value could have increased by $5 trillion.

Saylor emphasized that Bitcoin is a highly liquid and substitutable capital asset, which can serve as a modern alternative to bonds. Previously, in December 2024, Saylor had recommended that Microsoft adopt Bitcoin as a core corporate strategy. However, Microsoft shareholders voted against the Bitcoin investment proposal.

data.title: Michael Saylor again calls on Microsoft to buy Bitcoin to avoid capital losses
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Michael Saylor, chairman of the board of directors of Strategy (formerly MicroStrategy), once again called on Microsoft to adjust its capital allocation strategy and abandon bonds and stock repurchases in favor of Bitcoin, which he believes will create more shareholder value while reducing corporate risks. Over the past five years, according to TradingView data, Bitcoin's average annual return was 53%, while Microsoft's average annual return was about 6%; Bitcoin rose by more than 950%, while Microsoft rose by about 148%. Saylor emphasized that Bitcoin is a highly liquid and fungible capital asset that can serve as a modern alternative to bonds. (Cryptobriefing)

data.title: Grayscale transferred about 636 BTC to Coinbase Prime address, worth more than $61 million
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily reported that Arkham monitoring data showed that about 6 minutes ago, Grayscale Bitcoin Trust, a Bitcoin exchange-traded fund under Grayscale, transferred about 636.446 BTC to the Coinbase Prime address, worth 61.72 million US dollars; then it transferred another 310.476 BTC to the same address, worth 30.11 million US dollars.

data.title: Vaulta: EOS tokens are expected to become A tokens on May 14, with a 1:1 exchange rate
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Vaulta (formerly EOS) announced on the X platform that EOS tokens will become A tokens on May 14, with a 1:1 exchange ratio. There is no change in the token economics, no exchange fee, and the exchange can be completed through official channels on May 14.

data.title: Binance.US will launch AIXBT tomorrow, deposit channel is now open
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Binance.US officially announced that the AIXBT/USDT trading pair will start trading at 7 am (EST) on May 8. Currently, AIXBT deposits on Binance.US are open.

data.title: Kaiko: Bybit Bitcoin liquidity has returned to pre-hacker levels
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to the latest report from Kaiko, a crypto data provider, the Bitcoin liquidity of the Bybit exchange has returned to the level before the hacker attack last year. Kaiko pointed out that Bybit's BTC market depth has continued to improve in the past few months and has now reached the standard before the incident, indicating that the platform's market confidence and trading activity are recovering. According to Kaiko, Bybit's Bitcoin market depth has fully recovered from a low of 0.1% to 8%. The exchange has also outperformed its competitors after experiencing historic hacker attacks and uncertainty caused by tariffs. In March, competitors such as HTX, Bithumn and MEXC all saw double-digit percentage declines in liquidity. Kaiko also mentioned that although the overall market liquidity is still lower than the highs of 2021, Bybit's recovery is relatively fast, showing its competitiveness in the market. (The Block)

data.title: CMC Markets increases stake in blockchain startup StrikeX to 51%
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News - British online trading company CMC Markets has increased its stake in blockchain startup StrikeX Technologies from 33% to 51%, gaining majority control of the company and its technology roadmap. CMC first acquired a 33% stake in StrikeX in 2023, calling it a strategic investment in tokenized finance and self-custody technology. Today, StrikeX owns a majority stake and has officially become a subsidiary of the CMC Markets Group, enabling it to launch products faster, obtain regulatory approvals and expand globally. (Financefeeds)

data.title: Gemini Hires Brad Vopni as Head of Institutional
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Cryptocurrency exchange Gemini has hired Brad Vopni as head of institutions. Vopni will be responsible for overseeing and executing the company's institutional strategy. Prior to joining Gemini, Vopni served as head of digital assets at Hudson River Trading, one of the world's largest electronic trading companies, for 6 years, leading the company's expansion into the crypto field. (Coindesk)

data.title: Ethereum's Pectra Upgrade Raises Security Concerns
data.description: According to Odaily, former Immunefi smart contract and security engineer Vladimir has highlighted potential security risks associated with Ethereum's Pectra upgrade. He noted on the X platform that users can now fully withdraw funds by signing a single message. Vladimir emphasized the importance of exercising caution when signing transactions to prevent unauthorized access or potential losses.

data.title: Diversified Energy Faces Regulatory Challenges Over Abandoned Crypto Mining Site
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, cryptocurrency miner Diversified Energy has vacated a natural gas-powered mining site in Elk County, Pennsylvania, leaving behind unplugged wells and regulatory violations. The site, known as Longhorn Pad A, was reactivated in 2022 after nearly a decade of dormancy to power cryptocurrency mining computers. However, the operation commenced without an air quality permit from the Pennsylvania Department of Environmental Protection (DEP). Although the permit was granted in December 2023, a March 2025 inspection revealed that the mining infrastructure had already been removed. The DEP issued a formal violation notice for well abandonment, but Diversified denied abandoning the site, suggesting a potential resumption of gas production. Despite this, the DEP and environmental advocates argue that Diversified has not met its obligations. Under a 2021 agreement, the company committed to plugging Longhorn A and 13 other wells at the end of their operational life, a commitment reportedly unfulfilled.

Environmentalists have expressed concerns about Diversified's business model, which involves acquiring aging, low-producing wells and extracting remaining value without adequate decommissioning plans. Plugging a single well can exceed $100,000, and Pennsylvania already has over 350,000 orphaned and abandoned wells, raising significant concerns. A 2022 report criticized the company's approach as a "business model built to fail Appalachia," warning that taxpayers might bear the cost of unplugged wells. Diversified recently agreed to plug 3,000 wells by 2034 in a separate legal settlement but continues to face regulatory scrutiny, including a probe by the US House Committee on Energy and Commerce. Horton Township officials, where the Longhorn site is located, have received no updates from the company. Local supervisor PJ Piccirillo noted that generators and tanks were removed without notice, stating, "All we know is that the property seems to have been abandoned."

In a broader context, US cities are increasingly scrutinizing crypto mining operations. On April 25, the planning commission of Vilonia, Arkansas, unanimously rejected a proposal to establish a cryptocurrency mining facility within city limits, following strong resident opposition. In January, Arkansas lawmakers introduced a bill to ban crypto mining operations within 30 miles of any US military facility in the state. This opposition reflects a broader trend across various US municipalities where crypto-mining initiatives face increasing scrutiny. In October 2024, residents in Granbury, Texas, filed a lawsuit against Marathon Digital, alleging excessive noise from its mining facility.

data.title: Opinion: The Fed's cautious approach to rate cuts may not necessarily strengthen the dollar
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: MUFG Bank analyst Halpenny said in a report that if the Federal Reserve is cautious about the possibility of rate cuts in the coming months at its meeting on Wednesday, the dollar will not necessarily appreciate. He said that since the beginning of April, the euro has strengthened, even though the yield spread between the EU and U.S. two-year Treasury bonds has moved in favor of the United States. This means that cautious signals about rate cuts will not necessarily boost the dollar. Halpenny said that policy uncertainty and strong expectations that the U.S. economy will weaken mean that the dollar may remain weak or weaken further. (Jinshi)

data.title: Interview: Symbiotic’s Misha Putiatin on Shared Security Infrastructure and the Future of Decentralized Staking
data.description:
Symbiotic co-founder Misha Putiatin speaks to Cryptonews about raising $29M and building modular shared security infrastructure to simplify decentralization for emerging blockchain networks.
source: https://cryptonews.com/exclusives/symbiotics-misha-putiatin-on-shared-security-infrastructure-interview/
